question.title: How to write code for Selenium webdriver in Java for below HTML
question.body:
HTML:

<div id="Toolbar" style="color: rgb(35, 35, 34);">
    <ul class="dropdownMenu" style="display: none;"></ul>
    <div id="LeftToolbar" style="display: inline-block; width: 495px;">
        <ul class="dropdownMenu">
            <li>
                <div id="ScreenLayout" class="icon ScreenLayout group show" title="Screen Layout Selection">
                    <div id="ScreenLayoutIcon" class="icon show SCREEN1x1" title="Screen Layout 1x1" style="display: inline-block;"></div>
                    <div id="ScreenLayoutArrow" class="icon DROPDOWNARROW show" title="" style="display: inline-block;"></div>
                </div>
            </li>

I want to select "ScreenLayoutIcon" using Selenium + Java.

My code:

driver.findElement(By.id("LeftToolbar"));
driver.findElement(By.xpath("//*@id='ScreenLayout']//ul")).click();
List<WebElement> options = driver.findElements(By.tagName("li"));
for (WebElement option : options) {
    if (option.getText().equals("Screen Layout")) {
        option.click();
    }
}

answers.body:
There are a few issues.

  1. Your first line of code does nothing.

    driver.findElement(By.id("LeftToolbar"));

    You are fetching an element from the page but you don't do anything with it and don't store it in a variable.

  2. In the second line, the XPath is not valid syntax.

    //*@id='ScreenLayout']//ul
   ^ you are missing an '['

    It should be

    //*[@id='ScreenLayout']//ul

    BUT... there is no UL under the DIV that has the ID of 'ScreenLayout' according to the HTML you posted.

  3. Your loop is looking for the LI that contains the text "Screen Layout" but according to the HTML you posted, none of them do.

The only reference to "ScreenLayoutIcon" I see is the ID for this DIV

<div id="ScreenLayoutIcon" class="icon show SCREEN1x1" title="Screen Layout 1x1" style="display: inline-block;"></div>

If you HTML is correct (I suspect it is not), this should click the element you are asking for.

driver.findElement(By.id("ScreenLayoutIcon"));
